category_title: Students with learning disabilities who struggle with turn-taking and engagement in cooperative tasks
minimal_context: James is working on a cooperative group word-making lesson using index cards at his desk.
dialogue:
TUTOR: James, show me how to do the first part of the task with these index cards.

STUDENT: (Sits up in his seat, picks up an index card with the word 'out' printed on it, taps the card softly on the desk, and looks at the other cards on the table.)

TUTOR: (Takes the card and places it next to the word 'side'.) Now you do another one.

STUDENT: (Complies and correctly matches the words 'base' and 'ball'.)

TUTOR: Good job. Keep working with your group while I step away to check on the others.

STUDENT: (Slouches down in his seat and starts rolling a pencil back and forth across his desk. He watches the other children as they encourage each other to find word matches.)

options.A: Gives James positive attention and praise for listening, taking turns, and sharing with peers.
options.B: Allows James to withdraw from the group for a time-limited period of quiet time.
options.C: Pats James on the shoulder, smiles, and asks him to do another one.
options.D: Uses opportunities to respond to maintain James's orientation to the word-making instruction.
